rationale: Walked R1-R8: every requirement has an implementation AND a test pinning its falsification clause, none vacuous. R1 create.go co-bumps round+SHA in one Panel struct; TestCreate_WritesRegistrationAtomically + TestPanelCreate_StampsResolversAndCoBumpsRoundSHA guard the omit-SHA, no-re-resolve, verdict-file-untouched, and skill-body-untouched clauses; resolvers come from 109 config (asserted =3, "2"). R2/R3 verify+tally route through panel.PanelGateDecision; TestPanelVerbs_DecisionIsPanelGateDecision (13-row branch-complete table) + TestPanelStateVerdict_DelegatesToPanelGateDecision (delegation + source-text check the 4 old message literals are deleted) pin the single-home ratchet; verify writes-nothing pinned by tree snapshot; tally exit tracks decision incl stale-SHA/hard_block-despite-approves. R4 schema doc present, re-derived from its own tokens vs constants, section-scoped attack test. R5/R6 use the IDENTICAL loadImpactedDomains(contextpack.ParseSpec)+normalizeImpactedDomains(nil,root,"") and adrStoreForSpecFn store as plan.go, same impacted-domains-resolve code; bare-name and bare-prose tolerance both pinned; no coverage/irrelevant leaks. R7a: gate.go/panel.go untouched. R7b: go list -deps has no internal/config. R8 grep gates all green. Touched packages pass.
findings: R1: internal/panel/create.go Create() writes round+ReviewedHeadSHA into one Panel struct (co-bump by construction); marker-state validated before any disk write so a corrupt BRIEF leaves neither file changed. Falsification clauses (omit-SHA, no-re-resolve on re-panel, panel.json/BRIEF divergence, non-109 resolvers, prior-verdict-file overwrite, skill-body overwrite) each guarded by TestCreate_WritesRegistrationAtomically + cmd TestPanelCreate_StampsResolversAndCoBumpsRoundSHA.

R1 --bead/--target fail-safe is a reasoning property (divergent target -> stale-SHA false-BLOCK, never false-PASS), argued in code comments and doc; not a dedicated scenario test, but the staleness mechanism it relies on is directly tested. Acceptable — not a gap.

R2: cmd renderPanelVerify + instruct verdict() both call panel.PanelGateDecision; second matrix genuinely deleted — TestPanelStateVerdict_DelegatesToPanelGateDecision asserts the delegation call is present AND the four pre-refactor message literals are absent from panelstate.go source. Strong non-vacuous pin.

R3: tallyExitAction derives exit purely from Decision.Action; TestPanelTally_ExitCodeTracksDecision covers the lola-f4a8 class (approves satisfy threshold yet stale-SHA/hard_block must still Block+recovery-line via guard.HasFinalRecoveryLine).

R4: .mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md Panel Artifact Schema section present; TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants re-derives expectations from the doc's own backtick tokens vs panel.FileName/verdictFileRE/ConsolidatedName, and TestPanelSchemaDoc_SectionScoped_RejectsRenamedNormativeName pins the section-scope attack.

R5/R6: verified byte-for-byte parser/store parity — checkImpactedDomainsResolutionParity calls the same loadImpactedDomains (contextpack.ParseSpec) + normalizeImpactedDomains(nil,root,"",impacted) + impacted-domains-resolve code as plan.go:125-144; checkADRTouchpointsExist uses newMemoStore(adrStoreForSpecFn(root,specDir)), the identical store as checkADRCitations (plan.go:156). Bare-name-no-manifest tolerance and bare-prose ADR tolerance both pinned; digit-boundary regressions (5-digit no-truncate, no 4-digit-prefix false-pass) covered; assertNoCoverageOrIrrelevant on every R6 case.

R7a: internal/panel/gate.go and panel.go are NOT in the 2f894401..2da52b09 changeset — PanelGateDecision inputs/outputs unchanged. R7b: `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config` is empty. R7c: spec-approve emits no adr-coverage-missing (asserted).

R8: all six SKILL grep gates pass (decision-matrix table gone from ms-panel-tally, hand-typed reviewed_head_sha schema gone from ms-panel-run, both verbs referenced, Artifact gates + Slot lens defaults judgment sections retained).

INFORMATIONAL (defer to S3, not a change request): go test ./internal/instruct fails only on TestRun_IdleNoBeads — the known env-dependent z4ps pre-existing failure (renders the full instruct markdown on failure, dependent on the repo's live multi-spec state), NOT introduced by 110. All R2 instruct tests (TestPanelStateVerdict_DelegatesToPanelGateDecision et al.) pass; internal/panel, internal/validate, internal/approve, cmd/mindspec all green.
